hypothesis: If MatAgent's knowledge base were dynamically updated in real-time with LLM-generated explanations derived from each newly proposed composition-property pair encountered during iterative exploration—rather than relying on a static pre-constructed database of 10,000 random MP-20 composition pairs—then the framework would exhibit significantly accelerated convergence toward extreme target formation energies (e.g., the 1.0% quantile of −3.8 eV/atom) and improved V.U.N. scores, because the evolving knowledge base would increasingly capture composition-property relationships specifically relevant to the sparse, underexplored regions of materials space that static databases systematically underrepresent.
